transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Riverbend Health Line, this is Ava. What's going on today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Hi, um, I've had a really bad headache since yesterday and now my vision's a bit blurry.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm sorry you're dealing with that. Can I get your name and date of birth to pull up your file?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Sure, it's Marcus Doyle, born March 9th, 1985.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thanks, Marcus. Besides the headache and blurry vision, any nausea, weakness, or trouble speaking?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: A little nauseous, but no weakness or trouble talking.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay. Given the blurry vision with a sudden headache, I want you to be seen today rather than wait. Have you taken your blood pressure medication, the lisinopril, today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Actually no, I forgot it this morning.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That could be part of it. Please take it now, and I'm going to route you to an urgent care visit within the next two hours instead of the ER, unless symptoms worsen.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Okay, that sounds manageable. What counts as worse?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: If you get sudden weakness, slurred speech, or the headache becomes the worst you've ever had, go to the ER immediately. Otherwise, Lakeside Urgent Care on Fifth Street has an opening at 2 PM.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Great, I can make that. Thank you.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're welcome, Marcus. I'll send the appointment confirmation by text, and take your medication now. Feel better soon.
